items.title: Trump signs executive orders on quantum technology and cryptography
items.summary: President Trump signed executive orders addressing quantum technology and cryptography and commented on U.S.-Iran negotiations.
items.details: On June 22, 2026, President Trump signed executive orders related to quantum technology and cryptography. During the signing, he also commented on the U.S. negotiations with Iran, referencing an open strait and Iran not obtaining a nuclear weapon.

items.title: Executive order on advanced AI innovation and security issued
items.summary: The White House issued Executive Order 14409 directing federal agencies to strengthen cyber defenses and address risks from advanced AI.
items.details: On June 2, 2026, the White House issued Executive Order 14409, "Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security." It directs federal agencies to strengthen cyber defenses, establish a voluntary framework for secure frontier AI model deployment, and prioritize enforcement of criminal activity involving AI, with several actions required within 30 days.

items.title: Trump announces framework agreement with Iran
items.summary: President Trump said the U.S. and Iran reached a framework agreement to end the conflict and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
items.details: According to reports, the U.S. and Iran signed a preliminary agreement to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz. As of mid-June 2026, the terms of the deal remained secret, and lawmakers requested the agreement text and a briefing from the administration.

items.title: State Department announces visa restrictions and reward offers
items.summary: The State Department announced targeted visa restrictions related to Ethiopia and reward offers for information on MS-13 leaders.
items.details: On June 18, 2026, the State Department announced targeted visa restrictions for individuals it said were involved in undermining peace in Ethiopia. The department also announced reward offers totaling up to $15 million for information leading to arrests or convictions of leaders of the foreign terrorist organization MS-13.

items.title: Senate passes 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act
items.summary: The Senate passed the bipartisan housing bill H.R. 6644 by a vote of 85 to 5.
items.details: On June 22, 2026, the Senate agreed to a measure on H.R. 6644, the 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, by a vote of 85 yeas to 5 nays. The final language was agreed to by both House and Senate leadership following nearly a year of negotiation and includes provisions on community development funding and disaster recovery.

items.title: Senate rejects Iran war powers resolution
items.summary: The Senate narrowly rejected a motion to discharge an Iran war powers resolution in a 47-48 vote.
items.details: On June 16, 2026, the Senate rejected a motion to discharge a war powers resolution on Iran by a 47 to 48 vote. Four Republicans—Susan Collins, Bill Cassidy, Lisa Murkowski and Rand Paul—joined most Democrats in support, while Sen. John Fetterman was the sole Democrat opposed.

items.title: House passes Iran war powers resolution
items.summary: The House passed a resolution directing the president to end hostilities with Iran in a 215-208 vote.
items.details: On June 3, 2026, the House passed a war powers resolution by a vote of 215 to 208, with four Republicans joining Democrats. It marked the first time such a measure cleared the House on a final vote since the conflict began. The administration has questioned the constitutionality of the War Powers Act.

items.title: Supreme Court issues June 23 opinions
items.summary: The Supreme Court issued opinions in several cases on June 23, including Cisco Systems v. Doe and Pung v. Isabella County.
items.details: On June 23, 2026, the Supreme Court issued opinions in cases including Cisco Systems, Inc. v. Doe, Pung v. Isabella County, and United States v. Carter. The rulings came as the court worked toward concluding its term, with several major cases still pending.

items.title: Supreme Court allows Alabama to use redrawn congressional map
items.summary: The Supreme Court issued an order allowing Alabama to use a redrawn congressional map for the 2026 elections.
items.details: The Supreme Court issued an unsigned order allowing Alabama to hold its 2026 elections using a map that a lower court had found racially discriminatory. The order followed the court's ruling in Louisiana v. Callais, which raised the bar for proving Voting Rights Act violations. The map eliminates a majority-Black district.

items.title: Supreme Court rules in United States v. Hemani
items.summary: The Supreme Court ruled unanimously that prosecuting a marijuana user for gun ownership was inconsistent with the Second Amendment.
items.details: On June 18, 2026, in United States v. Hemani, the Supreme Court ruled that the government's prosecution of a marijuana user for owning firearms was inconsistent with the Second Amendment. Justice Gorsuch wrote that the decision was narrow and did not address bans on those presently intoxicated from possessing firearms.

items.title: Primaries held in New York, Maryland, Utah and South Carolina
items.summary: New York, Maryland and Utah held primaries and South Carolina held a gubernatorial runoff on June 23.
items.details: On June 23, 2026, New York, Maryland and Utah held primary elections while South Carolina held a Republican gubernatorial runoff between Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette and Attorney General Alan Wilson. President Trump issued a last-minute endorsement of both South Carolina candidates.

items.title: FBI searches Ohio voter registration group's offices
items.summary: FBI agents searched the Cleveland offices of the Ohio Organizing Collaborative as part of a Justice Department investigation.
items.details: In June 2026, FBI agents searched the Cleveland offices of the Ohio Organizing Collaborative, a voter registration and community organizing group, seizing computers and questioning staff as part of a Justice Department investigation into the group's voter registration efforts ahead of the midterm elections.

items.title: ACLU announces $50 million midterm election effort
items.summary: The ACLU said it will spend more than $50 million on the 2026 midterm elections, with about half on election administration.
items.details: The American Civil Liberties Union announced plans to spend more than $50 million on the 2026 midterm elections. About half will go toward ensuring smooth election administration, with the group training and deploying more than 100 paid staff and over 3,000 volunteer leaders to monitor ballot counting and certification.

MD.title: Maryland holds June 23 gubernatorial primary
MD.summary: Maryland held primary elections on June 23, with incumbent Gov. Wes Moore seeking a second term.
MD.details: Maryland's primary elections took place on June 23, 2026. Incumbent Democratic Governor Wes Moore is running for a second term. Early voting ran from June 11 to June 18, and the state offers same-day registration during early voting and on Election Day.

NY.title: New York holds state primary elections
NY.summary: New York held primaries on June 23 featuring contests for the legislature and Congress.
NY.details: New York's primary elections took place on June 23, 2026, with a governor's race atop the ticket. Contests included primary battles between incumbent Democratic members of Congress and challengers, as well as numerous state legislative races with open seats and incumbents facing challenges.

SC.title: South Carolina holds Republican gubernatorial runoff
SC.summary: South Carolina held a GOP gubernatorial runoff between Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette and Attorney General Alan Wilson.
SC.details: On June 23, 2026, South Carolina held a Republican runoff in the open race for governor between Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette and Attorney General Alan Wilson to succeed term-limited Gov. Henry McMaster. President Trump issued an endorsement of both candidates ahead of the vote.

VT.title: Vermont governor signs marijuana possession bill
VT.summary: Gov. Phil Scott signed S. 278, raising adult-use marijuana possession and sale limits.
VT.details: On June 22, 2026, Republican Gov. Phil Scott signed S. 278 into law, increasing the amount of cannabis retailers may sell in a single transaction and doubling how much marijuana an adult may legally possess in public. The new law took effect upon passage, allowing those 21 or older to possess up to two ounces.

NH.title: New Hampshire governor signs 54 bills into law
NH.summary: Gov. Kelly Ayotte signed 54 bills into law and issued several vetoes.
NH.details: During the week of June 19, 2026, New Hampshire Gov. Kelly Ayotte signed 54 bills into law on various subjects, including health benefit plan claims data and school administrative unit budgets. She also issued vetoes, including of bills related to historical markers and risk pool operations.

CT.title: Connecticut enacts youth online safety and AI law
CT.summary: Gov. Ned Lamont signed Public Act 26-15 establishing online safety protections and AI regulations.
CT.details: On June 2, 2026, Connecticut Gov. Ned Lamont signed Public Act 26-15, a bipartisan law addressing youth social media use, regulation of artificial intelligence, and workforce training initiatives. Officials said codifying the protections in state statute was necessary due to a lack of federal action.

IA.title: Iowa governor signs slate of bills into law
IA.summary: Gov. Kim Reynolds signed multiple bills on June 1, including age verification and civics education measures.
IA.details: On June 1, 2026, Iowa Gov. Kim Reynolds signed a number of bills into law, including measures requiring age verification for certain pornographic internet sites and establishing a seal of civics excellence program administered by the Department of Education. The governor had until June 2 to act on remaining bills.

CA.title: California uses voter-approved congressional map for 2026
CA.summary: The Supreme Court allowed California to use its new voter-approved congressional map in the 2026 election.
CA.details: The Supreme Court allowed California to use its new congressional map, enacted through voter-approved Proposition 50, in the 2026 election. The map was drawn to counter Texas's redistricting. Republicans had appealed after a federal court ruled against their challenge.

TX.title: Texas to use contested congressional map in 2026
TX.summary: The Supreme Court granted a stay allowing Texas to use its redrawn congressional map for the November election.
TX.details: The Supreme Court stayed a district court ruling that had found Texas's 2025 congressional map a racial gerrymander, allowing the state to use the map in the 2026 election. The map was redrawn following President Trump's call for Texas to add Republican-leaning districts.

OH.title: FBI searches Ohio voter registration group
OH.summary: FBI agents searched the offices of the Ohio Organizing Collaborative as part of a federal investigation.
OH.details: In June 2026, FBI agents searched the Cleveland offices of the Ohio Organizing Collaborative, seizing computers and questioning staff and volunteers across Ohio as part of a Justice Department investigation into the group's voter registration efforts ahead of the midterm elections.

GA.title: Georgia parties pick gubernatorial nominees
GA.summary: Georgia held a Republican gubernatorial runoff on June 16, with Rick Jackson advancing to face Democrat Keisha Lance Bottoms.
GA.details: On June 16, 2026, Georgia held a Republican gubernatorial runoff in which Rick Jackson defeated Lt. Gov. Burt Jones to become the nominee. He will face Democrat Keisha Lance Bottoms, who became the Democratic nominee in May, in the November election to succeed term-limited Gov. Brian Kemp.

disclaimer: This digest reports verified developments from cited news sources without endorsement of any party, candidate, or position.
